description:
The daemon's dispatch+recovery cycle has no backoff or retry limit. When a task's workflow fails immediately (e.g., runner can't start, agent crashes), the recovery logic cancels the workflow and resets the task to Ready. The same tick's dispatch logic then picks it up and starts a new workflow. This repeats every tick indefinitely, generating hundreds of failed workflows (211 failed vs 72 completed in current stats).

## Root Cause
In `daemon_scheduler_project_tick.rs`, `recover_orphaned_running_workflows` and `run_ready_task_workflows_for_project` both run in the same tick with no coordination. There's no per-task failure counter or cooldown period.

## Fix
1. Add `consecutive_dispatch_failures: u32` and `last_dispatch_failure_at: Option<String>` fields to the task JSON schema (protocol types).
2. In `sync_task_status_for_workflow_result` (daemon_task_dispatch.rs:317-471), when a workflow fails: increment the task's `consecutive_dispatch_failures` counter and set `last_dispatch_failure_at`.
3. In `run_ready_task_workflows_for_project`, skip tasks where `consecutive_dispatch_failures >= MAX_DISPATCH_RETRIES` (suggest 3). Auto-block them with `blocked_reason: "repeated_dispatch_failure (N consecutive failures)"`.
4. Reset `consecutive_dispatch_failures` to 0 when a task is manually set to `ready` via `apply_task_status` in task_shared.rs.
5. Add a cooldown: even below the max, don't re-dispatch a task that failed less than `min_retry_delay_secs` (suggest 60s) ago.

## Key Files
- `crates/protocol/src/lib.rs` — Task type definition (add fields)
- `crates/orchestrator-cli/src/services/runtime/runtime_daemon/daemon_task_dispatch.rs` — sync_task_status_for_workflow_result (increment counter)
- `crates/orchestrator-cli/src/services/runtime/runtime_daemon/daemon_scheduler_project_tick.rs` — run_ready_task_workflows_for_project (filter by counter)
- `crates/orchestrator-core/src/services/task_shared.rs` — apply_task_status (reset counter)
title: Add dispatch retry budget with auto-block to prevent daemon retry storms
